title: Guide to Using the Design Tab in SiteFlow
metaTitle: Guide to Using the Design Tab in SiteFlow
metaDescription: Upload design files, manage versions, add pins and comments, and approve or reject design versions in the SiteFlow project Design tab. Supports JPG, PNG, and PDF up to 100 MB.
author: SiteFlow Operations Team
body:
How to upload design files, manage versions, add comments and pins, and approve or reject design versions in the project Design tab.

Prerequisites

  • You must be inside a project
  • Only users with the Design permission enabled on their role can upload and manage design files
  • Only users with approval permission can approve or reject design versions

Design Tab Overview

Open a project and click the Design tab.

The Design tab has three sections:

Each file in the list shows its version number, approval status, who uploaded it, the number of pins, and upload date.

Supported File Types and Size Limits

Steps to Include a New Design File

  1. Click the Design tab inside the project
  2. Select the section: 2D Layout / Adoption, 3D Layout / Adoption, or Production Files
  3. Click + New File
  4. Click + Add
  5. Check the checkbox to select the file to upload
  6. Click Next
  7. Complete any remaining details and click Save

The file uploads and appears in the section with status Pending.

How to Upload a New Version

When a design file already exists and an updated version is ready:

  1. Open the design file from the Design tab
  2. Click Upload New Version
  3. Select and upload the new file

The version number increments automatically (V1, V2, V3, and so on). The new version shows Pending status until it is approved.

How to View and Switch Between Versions

  1. Click on a design file to open it in the viewer
  2. The viewer shows: version number, approval status, page count, and pin count
  3. To view a previous version: click the version number selector and choose a different version (e.g. Version 2)

All previous versions remain accessible. Version history is never deleted.

Steps to Include a Pin or Comment on a Design

Pins let you annotate specific areas of a drawing and notify relevant team members.

  1. Open the design file in the viewer
  2. Click anywhere on the drawing where you want to add a note
  3. Type your comment (e.g. “This doorway width needs to be revised”)
  4. To tag a team member: type @ followed by their name and select from the dropdown (e.g. @Ravi)
  5. Click Submit

The tagged person receives a notification. The pin appears as a marker on the drawing and is visible to all users with Design access.

Design File Statuses

How to Approve or Reject a Design Version

From the Approvals Panel

  1. Click the Approvals icon in the project header (top right)
  2. Click Design Version — a count shows how many are pending (e.g. “Design Version (3)”)
  3. Click on a pending item to open it
  4. Review the design
  5. Click Comment to add a remark before approving or rejecting
  6. Click Approve or Reject

Directly on the File

  1. Open the design file in the viewer
  2. Click the current status badge (e.g. Pending)
  3. Select the new status from the dropdown

Tips

  • Use version control instead of uploading over the same file. Uploading a New Version keeps all previous versions accessible, while the latest version is always shown by default.
  • Use @mentions in pins to notify the right person directly. A pin without a tag is visible but does not send a notification.
  • Check the Approvals panel regularly if you are an approver. The count badge shows pending items so nothing gets missed.
  • Each section (2D, 3D, Production Files) is independent. Upload drawings to 2D, models to 3D, and fabrication or shop drawings to Production Files to keep files organised.
  • The Files tab is for general project documents without version tracking. Use the Design tab specifically for drawings and models that need version history and formal approval.

If Something Goes Wrong
